steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: Before the blank: “their design” is a noun phrase positioned after a comma that separates it from the main independent clause. After the blank: “airflow, protection from insects, and hairstyle maintenance over comfort” are objects. The blank connects these two elements in the context of additional information following the main clause.

steps.title: Apply the absolute phrase rule
steps.content: An absolute phrase (noun phrase + participle) is the correct structure to modify a sentence when set off by a comma. Option C “prioritizing” creates this absolute phrase. Options A, B, and D use finite verb forms instead of a participle, violating this rule. That matches choice C — C is correct.
